title: Business Operations, Data and Financial Analyst
description:
We are a growing Australian healthcare and NDIS disability services group operating residential care, home care, respite and staffing services. Labour is our largest cost and service delivery is our biggest revenue driver — so understanding our numbers is critical. 



We are looking for a highly analytical Business Operations & Data Analyst to help us build visibility across revenue, labour cost, occupancy, and profitability.



This is NOT a data entry role and not a general virtual assistant role. We are looking for someone who enjoys working with numbers, building dashboards, and helping leadership make better decisions. 



Responsibilities



You will work directly with the business owner and senior managers to build and maintain operational reports, visualisations and dashboards and be responsible for extracting data, interpreting, visualising and then presenting to the team.



We need someone that can make the complicated uncomplicated and easy to understand, has a commercial focus, and can communicate clearly and visualise data and set goals. From the data, you will set goals. 



For example - someone that can come in, take a look at the cost of us sending work to agency, analyse the cost of our own staff, and then visualise the dollar figure of lost revenue last week/fortnight on agency cost. 



Your work will include:



- Overtime reporting including hours, dollar value, reason analysis and interpreting further (eg. x% of overtime was avoidable)

- Agency spend tracking

- Labour cost per service line (taking into account all of our expenses and determining the actual cost of hours sold)

- Bed occupancy %

- Care minutes invoiced vs paid

- Hours delivered by funding source and building breakeven points (eg. x hours sold to x organisation for breakeven)

- Setting goals for rostering staff to visualise how many hours need to be sold to increase revenue etc

- Breakeven modelling

- Building structured Excel or dashboards

- Presenting and developing clear summaries and reports with visualisations and charts for management to review 

- Setting KPIs and targets for staff based on data



(Our director runs a number of businesses and may request your assistance with other businesses for similar tasks also)



What we are looking for



- 4 years + experience in financial analysis, management reporting and operations analytics

- High proficient in excel (pivot tables, advanced formulas, dashboards)

- Understand labour-based businesses

- Be comfortable working with messy data and improving structure

- Be comfortable working with undeveloped systems and assisting to implement systems through your work 

- Be a strong, confident communicator that is highly experienced in your field

- Be able to provide examples of past work

- Ideally, someone with experience in an Australian market in the NDIS space 



The systems we currently use are Xero, Shiftcare, and Employment Hero, where most data will be kept. 



This is NOT a virtual assistant, data entry or social media role. This is an analytical, commercially focused role.



To apply, please
 us with your resume, a description of your past relative work, and attach a sample dashboard or report that you have created in the past.

title: Technical Customer Support
description:
Role title. Technical Customer Support.



Role summary.

You will provide technical support for FibreMax business voice and internet services, with a strong focus on VoIP and Australian NBN environments. You will own tickets end to end, keep customers updated, and work with suppliers to resolve faults.



What you will do.



1. Respond to support requests promptly, diagnose issues, and communicate the next steps clearly.

2. Troubleshoot VoIP issues, including registration, call routing, one way audio, call quality, and device setup.

3. Support NBN connectivity basics, including router setup, WiFi troubleshooting, and LAN configuration.

4. Provision and change services, extensions, numbers, call flows, IVR menus, queues, voi
 to
, and after hours routing.

5. Manage number porting tasks and service transfers, including checking forms and required details.

6. Escalate to suppliers with good diagnostics, follow up until resolved, confirm fixes with customers.

7. Keep accurate ticket notes, update documentation, improve runbooks and FAQs.



Must have skills and experience.



1. Experience in VoIP, ISP, PBX, or technical customer support.

2. Solid understanding of SIP basics, NAT, codecs, DIDs, and common PBX features.

3. Basic networking knowledge, IP addressing, DHCP, DNS, WiFi, and routers.

4. Clear written and verbal communication, strong attention to detail, calm under pressure.

5. Ability to work independently, manage priorities, and take full ownership of outcomes.



Nice to have.



1. Familiarity with Australian NBN processes and supplier fault handling.

2. Experience with PBX administration and handset support, Grandstream, Yealink, softphones.

3. Comfort reading logs, using portals, and working across multiple systems.



Work requirements.

Remote full time role aligned to AWST. Reliable internet, quiet workspace, and a USB noise cancelling headset.



Performance expectations.

Fast acknowledgement during rostered hours, high quality escalation notes, accurate provisioning, low ticket reopen rates, and regular documentation improvements.

title: Supply Chain Coordinator (B2B Specialist) – Offshore
description:
About Heroes

Heroes buys, operates, and scales baby and juvenile brands.



We are ambitious multi-brand developers combining operational excellence with customer-centricity to support parents and children through every milestone of their journey. Built by a team with a background in investment banking, venture capital, and D2C eCommerce, we have the right experience to nurture and grow brands and develop products that simplify, support, and enrich everyday family experiences.



At Heroes, we leave our egos behind, get our hands dirty, and push through obstacles to get things over the finish line. If this sounds like you, you should join us.





About the role

We are seeking a detail-oriented, proactive Supply Chain Coordinator to manage the end-to-end order flow for our UK B2B customer base. You will serve as the primary link between our customers’ portals, our internal systems, and our third-party logistics (3PL) partners.



The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced environment where accuracy is paramount. You won't just move data; you will monitor stock health, resolve logistical bottlenecks, and maintain excellent professional relationships with our commercial partners.



Responsibilities

Order Management: Daily monitoring of customer portals to download new purchase orders; manual processing of orders received via
.



System Synchronisation: Accurate uploading of all orders into our internal ERP/systems and external 3PL platforms to ensure seamless fulfilment.



Inventory Control: Downloading and reconciling daily inventory reports. Proactively reviewing stock availability and initiating stock transfers to prevent "out of stock" scenarios.



Customer Liaison: Acting as the first point of contact for B2B customers regarding order status, delivery issues, or shortages.



Stakeholder Collaboration: Working closely with the Commercial and Supply Chain teams to communicate inventory risks or system errors before they impact the customer.



Data Integrity: Maintaining 100?curacy in data entry to ensure UK retail compliance and avoid costly shipping errors or fines.



About you

Exceptional Attention to Detail: You are someone who spots a SKU error or a quantity discrepancy at a glance.



Data Proficiency: Highly skilled in data handling and spreadsheet management (Excel/Google Sheets).



Communication Skills: Native or bilingual-level written and spoken English, with the confidence to communicate professionally with UK-based corporate clients.



Technical Aptitude: Experience navigating B2B customer portals and ERP/Order Management Systems (OMS).



Reliability: Ability to work autonomously while maintaining strict daily deadlines tied to UK shipping cut-offs.



Desirable Experience

Previous experience supporting UK-based retail or wholesale businesses.



Familiarity with 3PL workflows and inventory transfer logic



Our benefits

15 days of paid vacation per year.



Remote Work



A standard 40-hour work week.



Please note: We require the successful candidate to align their working hours with the UK time zone. Monday – Friday (9:00 AM – 5:30 PM GMT).



Heroes is an equal opportunity employer looking to build a diverse, inclusive team. We hire based purely on business need, skillset, and competence and do not consider age, disability, sex, sexual orientation, pregnancy, maternity, race, religion or belief, or marriage or civil partnerships when making hiring decisions.

title: Operations Virtual Assistant
description:
Note: Interested applicants must submit their application exclusively through the OnlineJobs platform. Do not
 the company directly. Any candidate who contacts the company outside of the OnlineJobs system will be disqualified from consideration.



Operations Virtual Assistant

(Process-Driven | High-Precision | Behind-the-Scenes Operations)



Job Overview



Maid In America Cleaning Services is hiring an Operations Virtual Assistant to protect schedule integrity, data accuracy, and operational consistency.



This is a behind-the-scenes execution role. You will not be customer-facing. You will work inside defined systems and SOPs to ensure our operational engine runs cleanly and efficiently every day.



If you enjoy structured work, moving quickly without sacrificing accuracy, and taking pride in clean execution, this role will feel natural to you.



What You’ll Do:



1. Scheduling & Job Management (Primary Responsibility)

You will own scheduling preparation and system accuracy for our TN locations.



This includes:

- Creating and modifying clients, jobs, and visits in Jobber

- Preparing future-day schedules for review and approval

- Creating training tasks in Jobber for new tea
mbers

- Re-sequencing jobs and rebalancing teams within defined guardrails

- Maintaining week-to-week schedule consistency whenever possible

- Flagging and correcting conflicts or inefficiencies early

- Executing same-day scheduling adjustments exactly as directed



2. Data Accuracy & System Integrity

You will ensure operational data is accurate, complete, and reliable.



This includes:

- Updating customer notes, job frequencies, and service details

- Invoicing customers promptly after service completion

- Calculating cost-of-service data after jobs

- Preparing timesheet and tips reports for payroll

- Logging complaints and rectified cleans accurately

- Creating and publishing weekly operational reports

- Manual KPI data entry

- Verifying work-to-labor ratios using company tools



3. Administrative Support

You will support operational structure and remove administrative friction.



This includes:

- Supporting the Assistant Operations Manager with administrative execution

- Maintaining internal trackers and shared documents

- Following established documentation and checklists consistently



What This Role Requires

- High pace with high accuracy

- Comfort with structured, repeatable tasks

- Strong attention to detail

- Clear communication when issues arise

- Strong adherence defined systems and guardrails

- Ability to work independently within clear expectations



This role involves a high volume of operational tasks that must be completed correctly and consistently. As the company grows, volume will increase — and this role grows with it.



Who We’re Looking For



Detail-Oriented & Reliable

You take pride in doing things correctly and thoroughly.



High Throughput Executor

You can handle volume without losing structure or focus.



Process-Driven

You work well inside defined systems and appreciate clear expectations.



Accountable Communicator

You flag issues early, own mistakes quickly, and ask for clarification when needed.



Tech-Comfortable

You’re comfortable using tools like Slack, Google Suite, Chrome extensions, and scheduling platforms. Experience with Jobber is a strong plus.



Experienced in Fast-Paced Admin or Scheduling

Background in administrative, dispatch, logistics, scheduling, or operations-heavy roles is

preferred.



Work Requirements

- Monday–Friday, 8AM–5PM U.S. Central Time

- Reliable internet with backup

- Dedicated workspace

- Backup power options in case of outages

- Fluent written English



Our Core Values

- Communication

- Connection

- Quality

- Service

- Teamwork



If these values resonate with you, we want to hear from you.



Important Screening Step (Read Carefully)



SERIOUS APPLICANTS ONLY:

1. Complete our Culture Index survey: 

2. Submit your application with the subject line beginning with the core value you most resonate with (ALL CAPS).

3. Use a minimum of 5 Apply Points when applying

4. In your application message, include one sentence describing the most repetitive task you have performed at a high standard for 6+ months.



Applications that do not follow all instructions will not be reviewed.

title: Product Support Specialist (Open to Fresh Graduates)
description:
***FRESH GRADUATES WITH A DEGREE IN INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY, COMPUTER SCIENCE, OR A RELATED FIELD ARE ENCOURAGED TO APPLY AND WILL BE GIVEN PRIORITY.***



***TRAINING WILL BE PROVIDED***





About the Position

We are currently hiring a Product Support Specialist who is enthusiastic, reliable, and eager to learn.



This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving problems, working with technology, and assisting customers in a meaningful way.



Instead of simply responding with templated answers, you will take time to understand each customer’s business, their goals, and the challenges they are facing. The goal is to provide thoughtful, clear, and helpful solutions that truly support their needs.



We are looking for someone who is professional, curious, detail-oriented, and comfortable working in a fast-moving startup environment.





Key Responsibilities

• Provide timely responses to customer questions with clear and friendly communication

• Investigate and resolve technical or support-related issues

• Create and manage support tickets in Jira and coordinate with internal teams when escalation is required

• Review customer history and understand their business needs before providing solutions

• Deliver personalized and thoughtful assistance rather than relying on scripted replies





Qualifications

• Excellent English communication skills (both written and spoken) – required

• Should have basic knowledge or hands-on experience with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, or similar programming languages.

• Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving ability

• High level of attention to detail

• Ability to adapt conversations naturally instead of using scripts

• Positive attitude and willingness to learn new tools and technologies

• Customer-focused mindset with curiosity 



Preferred (Not Required)

• Previous experience in customer support, particularly in tech environments

• Experience using CRM/ticketing systems



Fresh graduates are welcome to apply. We provide training — what matters most is your communication skills, attitude, and willingness to grow.



Work Setup

• Remote / Work-from-home position

• 8 hours per day

• 5 working days per week (option to work 6 days if preferred)

• Full-time role





TO BE CONSIDERED, PLEASE FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY:



• USE “PRODUCT” AS THE SUBJECT LINE WHEN SUBMITTING YOUR APPLICATION

• ATTACH YOUR UPDATED CV/RESUME

• INCLUDE A SHORT COVER LETTER INTRODUCING YOURSELF



IMPORTANT: APPLICATIONS SUBMITTED WITHOUT A RESUME WILL NOT BE REVIEWED.

title: Technical Product Owner (WordPress + Scrum)
description:
**Must include a resume to apply. Applicants without a resume will be ignored. 



About the Role



The founder of P1peline currently acts as the Product Owner for two Scrum teams and is transitioning out of the day-to-day backlog management responsibilities.



The Technical Product Owner will work closely with the founder to translate product ideas, instructions, and documents from him into structured backlog items that the teams can execute.



The founder defines vision and priorities. Your role is to convert those ideas into epics, features, and user stories with clear acceptance criteria so developers can work efficiently. 



The primary purpose of this job is to save the founder time on writing the actual user stories and to allow more time to be spent clarifying each user story, prioritizing the backlog, and preparing user stories for future sprints. 



Over time, a strong performer in this role will almost certainly be trained and promoted into a full Product Owner position with greater strategic responsibilities and a significant pay raise. 



This role supports two teams:



1. App Development Team - Building the P1peline platform (currently based on WordPress with advanced plugins and integrations).

2. Service Team - Delivering websites and digital systems for clients and partner companies.



Key Responsibilities



1. Backlog Management

- Translate founder vision and instructions into user stories (often epics and features will be provided by founder) 

- Maintain, organize, and prioritize the backlog for two teams

- Refine user stories with clear context, acceptance criteria, and constraints



2. Sprint Preparation

- Work with Scrum Masters to prepare work before sprint planning

- Break down larger ideas into smaller tasks

- Help prioritize backlog items when needed



3. Ongoing Support

- Clarify requirements when developers and scrum master have questions

- Gather feedback from founders and stakeholders on progress 



Required Qualifications



- Strong experience working with WordPress development projects as designer, developer, or project manager 

- Experience working within Scrum / Agile teams and passion for Agile

- Experience writing user stories and acceptance criteria

- Comfortable communicating with founders, scrum masters, and developers

- Strong written English and attention to detail



Preferred Experience



- Experience in a Product Owner or Scrum Master role for app, web, or custom service teams

- Scrum certifications such as PSM or CSM

- Experience working with busy managers or founders and translating vision into action

- Experience with complex WordPress setups (plugins, integrations, automation)



User Story Examples:

- 

- 



This role is a good fit if you:

- Want to build a long-term career as a Product Owner

- Enjoy working in a startup environment where things move quickly

- Like collaborating closely with developers, Scrum Masters, and founders

- Are excited about working with a young, ambitious team primarily based in the Philippines

- Enjoy learning new systems, tools, and technical concepts

- Are interested in the technical side of product development, especially WordPress-based systems

- Are passionate about Scrum and Agile workflows

- Like taking large amounts of information and turning it into clear, structured user stories

 -Enjoy improving and optimizing backlog items so developers can execute efficiently



This role is not a good fit if you:

- Need constant direction or supervision to complete tasks

- Do not have experience with WordPress development environments or Scrum

title: Social Media Expert
description:
ARE YOU READY TO AWAKEN YOUR CREATIVE MIND?

Social Media Content Creator — UK Property Business

Part-Time | Long-Term | Real Growth Opportunity



We are a growing Scottish property business looking for a talented, passionate social media creator who wants more than just a job.

We are looking for someone who is ready to awaken their creative mind — to produce content that genuinely stops people scrolling, tells a story, and builds a brand people trust.

This role starts part-time. But if it suits us both, it grows — more hours, expanded responsibilities, the opportunity to manage a team, and with that, higher responsibility and higher income. We want someone who wants to grow with us.



DOES THIS SOUND LIKE YOU?

1.  Are you someone who is great at creating interesting, eye-catching content on social media?

2.  Are you confident creating static posts, reels AND carousels from scratch?

3.  Do you have strong video editing skills and love producing great reels?

4.  Are you experienced at posting on
, Instagram and LinkedIn?

5.  Do you take the time to understand a brand before you create content for it?

6.  Do you have hands-on experience creating content in UK real estate?

7.  Are you someone who works independently and takes real ownership of their output?

8.  Do you hit your deadlines — every time, without being chased?

9.  Are you detail-obsessed — checking grammar, accuracy and brand consistency as standard?

10.  Are you honest about your skills and fully committed to delivering what you promise?

If you answered yes — this could be the start of something great for both of us.



THE ROLE

Create and post content across
, Instagram, LinkedIn and then as time increases on to TikTok and YouTube — 3 hours per day, 5 days per week. You will submit content for approval initially, then post independently as confidence and trust builds. The goal is for you to own this entirely.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

• Produce up to 2 static posts and 1 reel / carousel every day — consistently and on time

• Create carousels, short-form video and branded graphics that look and feel professional

• Work within brand guidelines, using the logo, tone of voice and examples provided

• Source accurate, relevant UK property imagery — researched and realistic

• Submit content for review, then progress to posting independently

• Communicate proactively via Slack and flag any issues early



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

• Proven, demonstrable experience creating reels, carousels and static content

• Confident video editing skills — reels should be polished and engaging

• Experience creating content specifically within UK real estate

• A strong eye for detail — grammar, accuracy and brand consistency matter

• Genuine curiosity — someone who researches, looks at examples and thinks before they create

• Reliability and independence — someone we can trust to deliver without constant supervision

• Honesty about skills and experience from day one



THE OPPORTUNITY

This is not just a content role — it is the beginning of a career path for the right person.

• Start part-time and grow to full-time as the role develops

• Take on expanded creative responsibilities as the brand grows

• Progress to managing a small social media team

• Greater responsibility comes with greater income — we reward people who deliver



HOW TO APPLY

We want to see what you can do — not just read about it.

Record a 30-second reel on any UK real estate topic — it does not need to be perfect or even finished. We will not be using it to post. We are not judging the final product. We want to see you physically creating it — on screen, in the 
nt, showing us your process.

Use Loom or something similar to record yourself working. Show us what you do and how you think. Applications without this video will not be considered.



If you are creative, reliable, honest and ready to grow —

we would love to hear from you.

Let's build something great together.





THE OPPORTUNITY

This is not just a content role — it is the beginning of a career path for the right person.

• Start part-time and grow to full-time as the role develops

• Take on expanded creative responsibilities as the brand grows

• Progress to managing a small social media team

• Greater responsibility comes with greater income — we reward people who deliver





HOW TO APPLY

We want to see what you can do — not just read about it.

Record a short Loom video (5 minutes maximum) showing you creating the first 30 seconds of a reel related to Real Estate in UK or Scotland market.  Applications without this video will not be considered.



If you are creative, reliable, honest and ready to grow —

we would love to hear from you.

Let's build something great together.

title: eCommerce Web Designer — Shopify Store & Landing Pages
description:
vrsn is a Hong Kong-based footwear brand redefining what pain-relief shoes can be: functional, stylish, and built for everyday life. After a successful Kickstarter launch in 2024, we're scaling our eCommerce business globally at vrsn.co.



The Role



We're looking for an eCommerce web designer to design high-converting pages across our Shopify site. The majority of the work centers on the performance funnel — landing pages, product pages, cart experience, and other conversion-critical touchpoints — though the role extends to the broader site as well. You should understand how layout, hierarchy, and visual choices directly impact whether someone clicks, scrolls, or buys.



You'll deliver designs in Figma; we have a dev agency that handles implementation. This is an ongoing, part-time role with room to grow over time as the brand evolves.



What You'll Do



- Design landing pages, product pages, and other conversion-focused pages across our Shopify store

- Create designs with clear visual hierarchy, strong CTAs, and intentional information flow

- Produce variants for testing — different layouts, messaging emphasis, or page structures

- Collaborate with our marketing team to align designs with campaign objectives and brand guidelines

- Iterate based on feedback and performance data



What We're Looking For



- 3+ years of experience designing for DTC / eCommerce brands (in-house, agency, or freelance)

- Portfolio examples of conversion-aware eCommerce design — pages that look good *and* clearly drive action

- Solid understanding of how page structure, flow, and visual hierarchy affect user behavior and conversion

- Experience designing elements like comparison charts, social proof sections, benefit callouts, and other high-impact conversion components

- Strong skills in Figma and Adobe Creative Suite

- Able to take loosely defined briefs and deliver polished, clear page designs

- Organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable with quick turnarounds



Nice to Have



- Familiarity with CRO principles, A/B testing, and data-informed design iteration

- Experience working within Shopify themes or page builders

- Experience using AI tools (e.g., Midjourney, Adobe Firefly) to accelerate design workflows



To Apply



Please share:



- A portfolio showcasing eCommerce pages you've designed — landing pages, product pages, homepages

- A short note on how you approach designing for conversion



---



*Our team is primarily based in Hong Kong / APAC (GMT+8). Some overlap with these working hours is strongly preferred.*

title: Performance Media Buyer — Meta Ads (eCommerce / DTC)
description:
vrsn is a Hong Kong-based footwear brand redefining what pain-relief shoes can be: functional, stylish, and built for everyday life. After a successful Kickstarter launch in 2024, we're scaling our eCommerce business globally at vrsn.co.



The Role



We're looking for a performance media buyer to run and optimize our Meta ad campaigns. This role is equal parts execution and thinking — you'll handle day-to-day ad operations while also interpreting performance data, identifying what's working, and recommending what to test next. Meta's platform is evolving quickly, and we need someone who stays current with how the platform works today and adapts our approach as it changes.



We'll provide direction and context, but the best fit for this role is someone who's proactive — flagging issues before they're asked, suggesting optimizations, and helping us get smarter over time. This is an ongoing, part-time role with room to grow.



What You'll Do



- Analyze campaign performance and translate data into clear insights and recommendations — what's working, what's not, and what to try next

- Manage day-to-day ad operations: campaign setup, creative uploads, audience configuration, and troubleshooting

- Structure and run tests (creative, audience, campaign structure) and apply learnings to improve results over time

- Collaborate with the creative team to align media and creative strategy — surfacing what the data says about which creative is performing and why

- Provide clear, consistent reporting and flag opportunities or issues proactively



What We're Looking For



- 2–5+ years of experience running Meta ads for DTC / eCommerce brands (in-house, agency, or freelance)

- Strong understanding of Meta's current platform and how to work effectively with the algorithm (e.g., when to use CBO vs. ABO vs. Advantage+, how to structure for testing vs. scaling vs. retargeting)

- Strong analytical mindset; fluent in growth metrics (CAC, ROAS, LTV, Profit Margin %) and equally able to develop a point of view on *why* something is or isn't working and what to do about it

- Experience collaborating closely with creative teams on testing frameworks and feeding performance insights back into creative development

- Experience managing and scaling ad budgets in the five-figure monthly range or above

- Proactive communicator — comfortable making recommendations and working effectively in remote/async workflows

- Strong written English

- Detail-oriented and consistent — able to execute accurately while moving quickly



Nice to Have



- Experience with other paid platforms (TikTok, AppLovin, Google)

- Familiarity with measurement and attribution beyond last-click reporting



To Apply



Please share:



- A short note on your experience running Meta ads (in-house or agency), including the types of budgets you've managed

- Examples of accounts or campaigns you've worked on, including results (redact sensitive details as needed)



---

title: Workflow Automation Developer (Rewst | RMM/PSA)
description:
Starting Rate: $14/hour

Hours: 10 hrs/week to start

Working time: Flexible schedule 

Start Date: 2nd wk of March



Summary – We’re looking for a Workflow Automation Developer who loves building clean, reliable automations that reduce manual work for Managed Service Provider (MSP) operations. Your primary platform will be 
, and your first major focus will be building, improving, and documenting client onboarding and offboarding automation templates that integrate with common RMM/PSA ecosystems.



Please read all REQUIRED conditions below before applying: 

REQUIRED: You are comfortable using a time-tracking system and logging work accurately.

REQUIRED: You can attend a recurring stand-up call (time will be coordinated; schedule is flexible overall).

REQUIRED: You have hands-on experience building automations (Rewst preferred; alternatives acceptable if strong).

REQUIRED: You understand RMM/PSA environments (or have direct experience with one or more tools listed below).

REQUIRED: You have built custom workflows in Rewst (beyond installing pre-built crates) OR can demonstrate equivalent automation depth in another platform.



REQUIRED: You understand workflow execution patterns (triggers, branching/conditions, variables, parent/child workflows).

REQUIRED: You can design idempotent, re-runnable automations with clear success/failure paths and safe handling of destructive actions.



REQUIRED: You can implement robust reliability features: error handling, retries/backoff, partial failure recovery, and notifications.



REQUIRED: You have strong API integration skills: REST APIs, webhooks, OAuth/API key authentication, pagination, rate limits, async operations, and response validation.



REQUIRED: You can document workflows clearly (inputs, logic, exceptions, dependencies, and maintenance) and work independently while asking smart questions when requirements are unclear.

When you message us to apply, you MUST include the sentence below in the subject line or first sentence of your message. Applications that do not include this will be automatically discarded:

“I can work 10 hours per week, I’m comfortable using a time-tracking system, and I can attend the stand-up call. I am reliable, proactive, and willing to learn. My experience includes (list tools/platforms you’ve used)”



Also include: A link to your updated CV reflecting relevant workflow automation / RMM / PSA / API experience.



Responsibilities:

-Build, maintain, and optimize Rewst workflows for MSP operations.

-Create reusable, scalable client onboarding/offboarding automation templates (first priority).

-Integrate Rewst automations with RMM/PSA platforms (e.g., ConnectWise, Autotask, N-able, Kaseya).

-Connect systems using REST APIs, webhooks, and authentication methods (API keys/OAuth where applicable).

-Add error handling, logging, retries, and notifications to ensure workflows are reliable.

-Troubleshoot workflow failures, identify root causes, and implement permanent fixes.

-Secure credential and secrets handling with least-privilege principles.

-Write clear documentation for each workflow (purpose, triggers, steps, dependencies, and how to modify).

-Collaborate with the team to identify repetitive tasks and translate them into automation opportunities.





Preferred Experience & Skills:

-Direct experience with 
 (strongly preferred).

-Experience building custom workflows in 
 (beyond installing pre-built crates)

-Experience with MSP tooling and common integrations:

-PSA: ConnectWise Manage, Autotask, HaloPSA (plus)

-RMM: N-able, Kaseya, Datto RMM, NinjaOne (plus)

-Identity & security: Microsoft 365 / Azure AD (Entra ID), Google Workspace, SSO basics

-Experience implementing robust error handling: retries, backoff, partial failure recovery, and notifications.

-Multi-tenant MSP automation experience, including tenant isolation and client-specific overrides.

-Modular automation design with reusable components, versioning, and backward compatibility.

-Ability to safely deploy, update, and roll back workflows in production MSP environments.

-Scripting ability in PowerShell and/or Python (JavaScript/Node.js a plus).



Strong understanding of:

-API concepts (endpoints, pagination, rate limits, idempotency)

-Data transformations (JSON, mapping fields across systems)

-Basic IT operations processes (tickets, users, devices, groups, permissions)

-Workflow execution patterns: triggers, branching, variables, parent/child workflows



Stretch Goals / Responsibilities

-Build a library of standardized automation modules (naming conventions, templates, reusable components).

-Improve onboarding/offboarding beyond initial scope (license assignment, security baselines, device policies, backups).

-Create dashboards or reporting that show automation impact (time saved, error rate, success rate).

-Assist with automation design for additional MSP workflows:

-Ticket triage, user provisioning, password resets, alerts, device lifecycle changes

-Recommend improvements to current processes before automating (simplify ? standardize ? automate).



Keywords

Rewst, Workflow Automation, MSP, RMM, PSA, ConnectWise, Autotask, N-able, Kaseya, Datto RMM, NinjaOne, REST API, Webhooks, JSON, PowerShell, Python, Microsoft 365, Entra ID (Azure AD), Onboarding, Offboarding, IT Automation, Documentation, Integrations, Error Handling, Logging

title: Shopify CRO Audit — eCommerce Site & Funnel Review (DTC)
description:
vrsn is a Hong Kong-based footwear brand redefining what pain-relief shoes can be: functional, stylish, and built for everyday life. After a successful Kickstarter launch in 2024, we're scaling our eCommerce business globally at vrsn.co.



The Project



We relaunched our Shopify site in early 2026 and have been running paid traffic (primarily Meta) since. We've hit the big pieces, but we know there's a lot that's not optimized yet — and we want a sharp pair of outside eyes to tell us what to fix and why.



We're looking for a CRO / conversion optimization specialist to do a comprehensive audit of our site and performance funnels. This means looking at everything a customer touches from ad click to purchase: landing pages, product pages, collection pages, cart, checkout, and all the elements in between — layout, information hierarchy, CTAs, urgency cues, trust signals, pop-ups, hello bar, upsells, mobile experience, and more.



The deliverable is straightforward: a clear, prioritized list of what to change, what to fix, and what to test — with rationale based on your experience, not just best-practice checklists.



This is a fixed-scope project, but we're open to an ongoing partnership if the fit is right.



What We're Looking For



- Proven experience auditing and optimizing eCommerce / DTC sites on Shopify

- Deep understanding of performance funnels — how paid traffic flows through landing pages, PDPs, cart, and checkout

- Real CRO experience: you've run A/B tests, seen results, and can point to specific changes you've recommended that moved the needle

- Able to provide examples or case studies of previous audit work — we want someone who's done this before, not someone running our site through an AI checklist

- Strong ability to prioritize: not just "here are 50 things to fix" but "here are the 10 that matter most and why"

- Experience with post-launch optimization is a plus (new or recently relaunched sites)

- Clear communicator — deliverable should be actionable and easy for our team to execute against



To Apply



Please share:



- 1-2 examples or case studies of site/funnel audits you've done for eCommerce brands, including what you found and what impact the changes had

- A short note on how you'd approach auditing a recently launched DTC Shopify site running Meta traffic

title: Cold Email Specialist
description:
About the Role



We are hiring a Cold
 Specialist to build and manage high volume outbound
 campaigns across multiple businesses. This is not a generic VA role. You will own the entire cold
 engine: domain infrastructure, inbox management, campaign execution, deliverability, copywriting, and A/B testing. You will work directly with the founder and report on campaign performance weekly.



The right person for this role understands that sending cold
 at scale is a technical discipline. You know why you cannot send 500
s from a single domain. You know how to set up and warm secondary domains. You know how to write copy that earns a response, not just lands in an inbox.



What You Will Do

- Set up and manage fleets of secondary domains and inboxes (e.g. 
, 
, wearewiser.co) to enable high volume sending without damaging primary domain reputation

- Build, launch, and optimise cold
 sequences using SmartLead and/or Instantly, managing all inboxes through a unified master inbox view

- Use Clay to enrich prospect data, build targeted lead lists, and automate personalisation at scale

- Write compelling cold
 copy including lead magnets, calls to action, and multi step follow up sequences. You will be assessed on this during the hiring process

- Run structured A/B tests across subject lines, copy angles, offers, segments, and CTAs, then report on what is working and what is not

- Monitor deliverability metrics (open rates, reply rates, bounce rates, spam complaints) and take corrective action when numbers drop

- Manage domain warming schedules and inbox rotation to maintain sender reputation across all active domains

Coordinate with the founder on targeting strategy, campaign priorities, and response handling workflows



Required Skills and Experience

- You must have hands on experience with all three of these tools. This is non negotiable:

- Clay: lead enrichment, data workflows, automated personalisation

- SmartLead: multi inbox campaign management, inbox rotation, unified reply tracking

- Instantly:
 sequencing, domain warmup, deliverability management



Beyond the tooling, you need to be a strong cold
 copywriter. Understanding the technical infrastructure is one skill set. Writing copy that gets replies is another. We need both. If you only know the tools but cannot write, or you can write but have never managed domains and inboxes at scale, this is not the right fit.



Nice to Have

- Experience managing 10+ domains and hundreds of inboxes simultaneously

- Familiarity with lead magnet strategy (offering free value to generate initial positive responses before escalating to calls)

- Experience working with SDR teams or building response handling workflows

- Understanding of DNS records (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) and
 authentication

- Previous agency or freelance cold
 experience across multiple clients or industries



How We Will Assess You

As part of the application, you will be asked to complete a short practical assessment. This will include writing sample cold
 sequences for a real business scenario, including a lead magnet approach and a direct CTA approach. We will evaluate your copywriting, your understanding of sequencing and follow ups, and your ability to think strategically about targeting and offers.



How to Apply

In your application, please include:

- A brief summary of your cold
 experience, specifically mentioning Clay, SmartLead, and Instantly

- The largest number of domains and inboxes you have managed at one time

- An example of a cold
 campaign you ran that performed well, including the metrics (reply rate, positive response rate) and what made it work

- Your availability to start and your preferred working hours (UK business hours preferred)

- Generic applications that do not address these points will not be considered.

title: STR Maintenance Coordinator
description:
The Brighthouse is growing fast, and our field team can't do it all alone. That's where you come in.



We're hiring a remote Maintenance Coordinator to work hand-in-hand with our on-the-ground teams. You won't do the repairs — but you'll make sure they get done, completely and correctly, without being reminded. You're the air traffic controller behind the scenes. Nothing lands without your confirmation.



What You'll Be Doing:



Monitor open maintenance issues and track every one to completion

Follow up with vendors until work is confirmed done — with photos, notes, and receipts to prove it

Coordinate with the field team to schedule walkthroughs and repairs

Organize job logs, before/after photos, and receipts in 

Assist with weekly punch lists, property logs, and project boards

Research and vet new vendors when needed



A Bit About You:



You have experience in property management, STR, or a similar field

You close loops. You don't wait to be reminded. You don't ask permission to do something obvious.

You're comfortable in Slack, spreadsheets, and 

You communicate clearly, track details obsessively, and treat "done" as meaning fully done — logged, confirmed, and closed

You ask good questions, solve problems all the way through, and take pride in doing things right the first time

When something isn't clear, you say so. When something is clear, you act on it.



One thing that will not work here: outsourcing your thinking to your manager. We'll give you SOPs, training, and support — but we expect you to use your own judgment and own your work.

The Job:



Full-time, 8 hours per day, Monday through Friday

Shift: 8 AM to 5 PM Eastern Time (includes 1 hour break)

Starting pay: $3.50 USD/hour

13th month pay included

First raise at 6 months, second at 12 months — both based on performance

One full week of paid hands-on training before your regular shift begins



How to Apply:

 
 with the subject line "I want to be your maintenance coordinator." Include a link to your OnlineJobs.ph profile. We'll send you a Google Form — fill it out completely and carefully. When asked your age, answer "44." This tells us you read instructions. Only complete applications will be considered.



One more thing: This is a full-time role that requires your full attention during working hours. If you are currently working another job during these hours, or plan to, please do not apply. We will ask about this directly, and honesty is required.



This is not a low-accountability role. We have high standards, detailed systems, and a small team where every person's output matters. If you're looking for a job where you can coast, this isn't it. If you want to be part of a high-performance team, grow your skills, and do work you're proud of — we'd love to hear from you.

title: Reddit Assistant
description:
We’re hiring people to manage daily activity on Reddit, including posting content and interacting within communities. This is ongoing work and requires someone who actually understands how Reddit functions, not just how to scroll it.



You’ll need atleast 2+ devices (Android only). If you own multiple devices, that’s a plus and allows you to earn more. Expect roughly one hour of work per device each day.



Each day you’ll post content across several subreddits.



A critical part of this role is reading the room. The videos we provide can range from humorous to emotional to awkward or uncomfortable. You need to instantly recognize the tone and write posts that feel natural for that type of content. If you post everything the same way, you’ll fail.



You’ll also be responsible for finding appropriate subreddits for each video. That means reviewing community rules, understanding what performs well there, and making sure your post doesn’t look forced or out of place. If you can’t do that consistently, this position isn’t for you.



We’re looking for people who think independently, adapt fast, and don’t need their hand held. Prior Reddit experience is helpful but not mandatory if you learn quickly.



This is a long-term opportunity for dependable workers. Compensation is solid and can increase based on results and how many devices you use.



Earnings:

Most contributors earn $100–$500 per month, depending on consistency and the number of devices used.



Time Commitment:

Daily time commitment ranges from about 1 to 5 hours, depending on how many devices you’re using



To apply, include:

• how many devices you can work with

• your experience using Reddit

• when you’re available each day

title: Facebook Ads Specialist + Technical Lead
description:
Location: Remote | Full-Time | Strong Overlap with Eastern Hours



Compensation: Fixed monthly salary (USD), based on experience and location, with performance-based bonuses. This is a full-time, long-term role.



Why This Role Rocks: 



This is not a “run ads and send reports” position.



As our
 Ads Specialist + Technical Lead, you’ll own the entire paid growth engine that fuels Podcast Your Brand’s revenue. Your work directly impacts pipeline, sales velocity, and company growth by consistently generating 5–10 qualified discovery calls per week.



You’ll work closely with our CEO and creative team to design, launch, test, and scale full-funnel campaigns - from ads and landing pages to
 automations and tracking infrastructure.

If you love performance marketing, thrive on numbers like CPL, ROAS, and booked calls, and enjoy building systems that actually convert, this role was built for you.



Your Mission:



Drive consistent, high-quality sales discovery calls (5–10 per week) through paid ads,
 marketing, and technically sound funnel execution.



The Role:



As our
 Ads Specialist + Technical Lead, you’ll own and optimize our paid marketing ecosystem end-to-end.



This role sits at the intersection of:

- Performance marketing

- Technical funnel implementation

- Analytics and optimization



You’ll be responsible not only for strategy, but also for hands-on execution across
 Ads, Google Ads, landing pages, automations, and tracking systems.



Responsibilities:



Paid Media Management

- Own
 Ads strategy, setup, testing, and scaling ($3K–10K/month initially, scaling to $20K+)

- Manage and optimize a targeted Google Ads account focused on discovery call generation

- Run ongoing A/B tests across audiences, creatives, offers, and funnels

Funnel & Technical Execution

  - Clickfunnels

 & Content Marketing

 outreach initiatives (when applicable)

 Page posts per week aligned with campaign goals



Creative & Team Collaboration

- Provide performance feedback to guide future creative direction



Client Campaign Support

- Assist with technical setup and integrations for client lead-generation
 sequences

Must-Have Experience:

- 3+ years of experience running paid media campaigns 
 required; Google Ads preferred)

   -
 Ads Manager

   - Clickfunnels

   - WordPress (Elementor)

   - GoHighLevel

Bonus (but not required):

- Experience supporting client-facing growth campaigns



What we offer:

- Competitive Monthly Pay: we are looking for the right person to work with us long-term. Pay will be competitive for the role as well as a lucrative bonuses in place for the team (That you - can participate in) as well as bonuses individually

- Full-time position with BONUS: This is a full-time position so we need this to be your biggest and primary priority from a work stand-point. Give us your best and we can work to give you bonuses along the way for helping the company hit our goals.

- Flexible Schedule: Work remotely with autonomy, as long as you’re available during core Eastern Time Zone hours for meetings and calls.

- Long-Term Growth: We are looking for a tea
mber who can grow with us and play an increasingly integral role in our day-to-day production dept.

- Collaborative Team Culture: Work directly with our CEO and creative team, producing content for high-caliber clients.



About Us:



Podcast Your Brand is an industry-leading podcast agency helping B2B founders, attorneys, financial experts, and entrepreneurs create profitable podcasts that generate leads, build authority, and grow their businesses.



Our clients are proven, successful, high-trust leaders who care deeply about impact, content, and credibility.



We’re growing quickly, and we need a creative, detail-oriented video editor who can bring long-form podcast and thought leadership content to life, transforming raw footage into engaging videos optimized for YouTube and beyond.



Our Culture and Values:



At Podcast Your Brand, we believe in fostering a culture of excellence, accountability, and creativity. We align ourselves with the following principles:

- Don’t take anything personally.

- Always do your best.



The Four Referability Habits:

- Say please and thank you.



Core Values:

nts.

title: Replo Developer (Shopify) – Long-Term Role
description:
We are a fast-growing DTC e-commerce company launching multiple brands across different niches. Our design team provides fully completed Figma designs, and we’re looking for a technical Replo Developer who can accurately bring those designs to life.



This is strictly a development-focused role. We are not looking for a designer—we need someone who focuses on clean builds, precise spacing, typography, and perfect mobile responsiveness.



..



Job Details



Rate: $12–$15/hour (depending on experience)

Hours: ~20 hours per week

Type: Long-term ongoing work

Projects: Landing Pages, Product Pages, and Homepages



..



Key Responsibilities



- Build high-performing Shopify pages in Replo based on provided Figma designs.

- Ensure the final page matches the design exactly, with correct spacing, typography, and responsiveness across all breakpoints.

- Develop reusable components and templates using Shopify Metafields and dynamic data where applicable.

- Structure pages efficiently to maintain fast loading speeds and clean builds.



..



Requirements



- Ability to build custom page layouts from scratch in Replo without relying on pre-built templates.

- Comfortable inspecting Figma files for exact measurements, assets, and CSS properties.

- Understanding of how Replo integrates with Shopify, including working with metafields and dynamic content. Experience with Liquid is a plus.

- A careful approach to development with strong attention to mobile responsiveness and layout precision.



..



How to Apply

Please use “REPLO-DEV” as your subject line and answer the following questions:



1. Please provide 2–3 links to live Shopify pages that you personally built using Replo.

2. Do you typically build pages manually in Replo, or do you use auto-import tools/plugins? (We require manual builds to maintain a clean structure.)

3. Can you confirm your availability for approximately 20 hours per week for long-term work?

title: Wordpress Developer
description:
DMN8 Partners is seeking a highly skilled and reliable WordPress Developer based in the Philippines to join our team. This role is focused on the critical "under-the-hood" health of our web properties. If you are obsessed with Core Web Vitals, site hardening, and the nuances of the Divi ecosystem, we want to talk to you.



Role Overview

Location: Remote (Philippines-based).



Hours: Part-time (20 hours per week).



Schedule: Flexible daily task completion (Mon–Fri), but must be available for emergencies during US Eastern Standard Time (EST) business hours.



Compensation: Competitive, based on experience.



Primary Responsibilities

Your main objective is to ensure our websites are fast, secure, and running without a hitch.



Speed & Performance Optimization: Audit and improve load times, optimize images, manage caching layers, and ensure high scores on Google PageSpeed Insights.



Security Management: Proactive monitoring, malware scanning, and implementing security best practices to prevent vulnerabilities.



Divi Mastery: Maintain and troubleshoot sites built on the Divi theme/builder, ensuring design integrity while optimizing for performance.



Plugin Management: Responsible for updating, auditing, and vetting plugins. You should know when a plugin is necessary and when it's just "bloat."



Daily Maintenance: Execute a daily checklist of site health tasks to ensure 100% uptime and functionality.



Requirements & Qualifications

Expert WordPress Knowledge: Deep understanding of the WP core, database optimization, and file structure.



Divi Power User: You must have extensive experience working with the Divi theme and builder.



Technical Proficiency: Strong understanding of HTML/CSS is highly preferred to make manual adjustments when builders fall short.



Availability: While you manage your own hours, you must be available to respond to emergency "site down" or critical security alerts within 2 hours during EST business hours.



Reliability: Proven ability to manage a daily task list independently without constant oversight.



Why Work With DMN8 Partners?

We value technical excellence and autonomy. You won't be micro-managed on when you work, as long as the daily tasks are completed, the sites are lightning-fast, and you are there when the "smoke alarm" goes off.



To Apply: Please submit your portfolio or list of sites you currently manage, specifically highlighting your experience with Divi and any performance benchmarks (before/after) you’ve achieved.



Note: A brief technical test involving a Divi performance audit may be required as part of the interview process.

title: Meta Ads Media Buyer - Lead Gen - Up to $3,000/month (Remote)
description:
We are looking for a highly skilled Meta Ads Media Buyer to join our growing lead generation agency.



Our agency specializes in running high-performing Meta advertising campaigns for construction and home-service companies in the Netherlands. The goal of every campaign is simple: generate high-quality leads that convert into real customers.



Because of the responsibility of this role, we are specifically looking for someone with real hands-on experience running Meta Ads campaigns, preferably within lead generation.



This is not an entry-level role. We are looking for someone who understands Meta Ads deeply, can think creatively, analyze data, and continuously improve campaign performance.



About The Role



As a Media Buyer, you will be responsible for managing and optimizing 30–40 Meta Ads accounts for our clients.



You will monitor performance daily, test new creative concepts, and identify optimization opportunities across multiple accounts.



This role requires someone who can work independently, think strategically, and react quickly when campaign performance changes.



You will work closely with our internal team while maintaining ownership of campaign performance.



What You Will Be Doing



• Managing and optimizing Meta Ads campaigns for lead generation clients

• Monitoring campaign performance on a daily basis

• Analyzing key metrics such as CPL, campaign stability, and creative performance

• Testing new ad creatives and identifying winning concepts

• Diagnosing performance issues and implementing optimizations

• Managing multiple accounts while maintaining stable results



Our Clients



All of our clients operate in the construction and home-services industry in the Netherlands.



Examples include:



• Roofing companies

• Solar installation companies

• Window replacement companies

• Renovation contractors

• Bathroom renovation companies



The goal of every campaign is simple: generate qualified leads that convert into real customers.



Requirements



To qualify for this role you should have:



• Proven experience running Meta Ads campaigns

• Experience with lead generation campaigns

• Strong understanding of campaign structure and optimization

• Experience managing multiple ad accounts simultaneously

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

• The ability to diagnose performance changes quickly



You should be comfortable working with campaign performance data and making decisions based on numbers.



Ideal Candidate



We are looking for someone who is creative, analytical, and highly independent.



The ideal candidate:



• Is highly creative with ad concepts, hooks, and messaging

• Understands how to test and identify winning ad angles

• Can analyze campaign performance and react quickly

• Is organized and structured when managing multiple accounts

• Can work without constant supervision



We are not looking for someone who needs instructions for every small change.



We are looking for someone who:



• Takes ownership of their work

• Solves problems independently

• Continuously improves campaigns

• Enjoys testing and optimizing ads



Working Environment



This is a remote position.



Our entire team operates within the Amsterdam / Central European Time zone (CET).



Because all of our clients are based in the Netherlands, you must be comfortable working within this time zone.



Compensation



Compensation depends on experience and performance.



Media buyers in this role can earn up to $3,000 per month or more depending on experience and results.



This role also offers long-term growth opportunities as our agency continues to scale.



Hiring Process



Our hiring process consists of three steps:



Application Form



Interview Call



Practical Skill Test



We carefully review every candidate to ensure we hire experienced media buyers who understand performance marketing and lead generation.



Apply Here



If you believe you are a strong fit for this role and have real experience running Meta Ads campaigns, please complete our application form below.



Apply here:





Please take your time when completing the form.

title: Reliable Personal Assistant to UK CEO
description:
30 apply points required for job role

DO NOT APPLY FOR THIS JOB WITH LESS THAN 25 PINTS PLEASE YOU WILL AUTO BE DELETED 



==============================================================================================

Full-Time | Immediate Start | Long-Term Position



We are a UK-based accounting and financial services firm seeking a highly disciplined, experienced Personal Assistant to directly support the CEO.



This is a serious, high-responsibility role.

If you are not experienced in executive-level support, do not apply.



?



Working Hours



Monday to Friday

9:00am – 5:00pm (UK Time)

Remote – Professional Home Office Required



Must be able to start within 7 days.



?



Mandatory Requirements (Non-Negotiable)



• Minimum 3+ consecutive years as a Personal Assistant / Executive Assistant

• Experience supporting a Director, CEO, or Senior Executive

• Fluent, professional English (spoken and written)

• Strong Excel / Google Sheets capability

• Experience managing calendars, reports, and confidential information

• Ability to work independently without constant supervision



If you are currently working another full-time job during these hours, do not apply.



If you cannot provide verifiable previous employer details, do not apply.



?



Core Responsibilities



• Managing and protecting CEO calendar (zero scheduling errors)

• Drafting professional
s on behalf of CEO

• Tracking KPIs and preparing structured reports

• Managing task lists and enforcing accountability

• Coordinating recruitment interviews and shortlisting

• Tracking invoice follow-ups and financial updates

• Preparing meeting summaries with clear action steps

• Acting as operational gatekeeper for CEO



Accuracy, discretion, and reliability are critical.



?



Performance Expectations



You will be evaluated on:



• Response time

• Accuracy

• Report quality

• Communication standards

• Reliability and discipline



This is not a casual assistant role.

You will be measured on performance.



?



Confidentiality & Compliance



NDA required.

Background verification checks will be conducted.



We operate with sensitive financial information. Confidentiality is non-negotiable.



?



Compensation



Competitive hourly rate based on experience. $4per hour increasing to $5-6 per hour after month 3

Long-term growth opportunity for the right candidate.



?



Application Instructions

THIS JOB IS HARD.

THIS IS NOT FOR THE AVERAGE ASSISTANT.



We are hiring experienced, mature, degree-qualified professionals only.



No young graduates.

No entry-level applicants.

Reliable, disciplined individuals only.



This role requires structure, accountability, and executive-level thinking.



There is a 3-stage interview process.



If you are not prepared for a serious, professional hiring process, do not apply.



?



TO BE CONSIDERED, YOU MUST INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING:

WOMEN ONLY OVER 30 YEARS OLD WE HAVE ENOUGH MEN ON OUR TEAM ALREADY , AND WE DONT HIRE NEW GRADUATES WE DONT HAVE TIME PEOPLE WITH LIFE EXPERIENCE ONLY

How you apply says a lot about you.



Careless or incomplete responses will not be reviewed.

 1. Company worked for in 2024 and 2025

 2. Current job you are doing today

 3. Exact daily responsibilities (clear bullet points)

 4. Your direct contact details 
 + WhatsApp)

 5. A 
 video link answering questions 1–3



Do NOT contact us unless questions 4 and 5 are completed.



Applications with vague, generic, or unserious answers will be rejected immediately.

Start your application with: STRICT/PA26



Applications that do not follow instructions will not be reviewed.
salary: $4 p/h starting After Inc $5–$6 per hour

title: Experienced Real Estate Cold Caller/Lead Setter
description:
Do you have an excellent speaking voice? Are you an excellent appointment setter? Do you enjoy this sort of position?  An established real estate Wholesaler & Disposition Firm has a home for you.   You can work part-time or full-time, and you'll  have experience in using a dialer.  

This is an important position with room to grow and learn the various stages of this industry.  Bonus for those who take this position seriously. Include I'M Serious!  in the response

Send a resume and a 2-3 audio clip of you speaking as if you're introducing yourself while addressing questions the Seller may have, and how you handle and take the calls.

title: Amazon Operations VA (FBA & OA | Full-Time | EST)
description:
We are an established Amazon Online Arbitrage (OA) business operating in the US marketplace.

This is a long-term, full-time role with clear SOPs, defined responsibilities, and consistent biweekly pay.



What This Role Is



-You will follow clear SOPs

-You will execute defined daily tasks

-You will report work clearly and consistently

-You will work inside a structured Amazon operation



Core Responsibilities



-Product sourcing (Online Arbitrage lead generation)

-Secondary Responsibilities (SOP-Driven)

-Upload sourcing leads into live Google Sheets

-Handle Amazon ungating submissions (templated)

-Create and follow up on reimbursement cases

-Manage returns and refunds (execution only)

-Draft buyer message responses

-Prepare A-to-Z cases for approval

-Handle inauthentic complaints

-Maintain accurate case logs and documentation

Note: Product sourcing is the primary task. All other tasks are secondary and SOP-based.



Requirements (Please Apply Only If You Meet These)

- 1+ years of Amazon Seller Central experience

- Strong experience with Online Arbitrage

- Daily working knowledge of:

- Amazon Seller Central

- Keepa

- Sellerboard

- Google Sheets

-Slack



Hands-on experience with:

-Ungating categories and brands

-FBA operations and reimbursements

-Able to work 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST (night shift in PH)

-Highly detail-oriented and able to follow SOPs exactly





Application Instructions (very important)



(To respect both your time and ours)



We use a short video to avoid long interviews and to identify serious applicants.



Step 1 — Record a Short Video ( no longer than 90 seconds)



Camera on. Answer in order:



1. How do you usually handle ungating categories or brands on Amazon?



2. How do you identify, document, and claim lost or damaged FBA inventory?



3. What Amazon tools do you use daily in your work?



Step 2 — Upload to Google Drive



Create one Google Drive folder containing:



Your video



Your resume (PDF preferred)



Set permissions to “Anyone with the link can view.”



Applications missing the video, answers, or resume will not be reviewed.

title: Bookkeeper
description:
STRbkeep is a fully remote bookkeeping practice based in Texas. We specialize in Short-term rental (STR) property owners and hospitality service providers across the United States. We currently manage 45+ QuickBooks Online accounts and are growing. The founder handles all client relationships and review work — we're hiring our first tea
mber to help with the day-to-day bookkeeping execution.



We need a detail-oriented bookkeeper to handle data entry, bank reconciliations, and transaction reclassification in QuickBooks Online. Work is assigned in batches with clear task lists and deadlines. You follow SOPs precisely, notice when something looks off, and communicate clearly rather than guessing. If something is unclear, you ask specifically and early — before it becomes a problem.



This is a full-time remote position with a flexible schedule.



Position Details

Type: Independent contractor, remote

Hours: Full-time, 40 hours per week

Pay: $7–$9/hr USD based on experience

Schedule: Flexible — you set your own hours and deliver by agreed deadlines

Overlap: During onboarding, we'll need 2–3 hours of overlap during US Central Time business hours for task handoffs, walkthroughs, and Q&A. Once you're comfortable with the workflows, the schedule becomes more flexible.



Daily Responsibilities

- Categorize and enter transactions in QuickBooks Online for multiple client accounts following provided SOPs

- Complete monthly bank and credit card reconciliations using provided checklists

- Reclassify miscategorized transactions based on client-specific rules

- Flag discrepancies, missing receipts, or anything that looks inconsistent before proceeding

- Send a brief daily update summarizing completed tasks and any questions



Requirements

- QuickBooks Online proficiency — categorization, bank feeds, and reconciliations

- At least 1 year of experience with data entry and transaction categorization across multiple accounts

- Basic to intermediate Excel or Google Sheets skills

- Fluent written and spoken English

- Reliable computer and stable internet connection

- Dependable — meets deadlines and communicates proactively



Who Does Well in This Role

- This role is about consistency and precision. You'll do well here if you are:

- Detail-oriented — you catch the small things others miss

- A fast learner — you pick up new processes quickly with minimal hand-holding

- Reliable — you show up on time and follow through on commitments

- Process-driven — you work from SOPs and checklists without skipping steps

- A clear communicator — you ask questions when something isn't clear rather than guessing



Nice to Have

- Familiarity with Airbnb, VRBO, or other short-term rental platforms

- Experience working with US-based clients or a US-based bookkeeping/accounting firm

- QuickBooks Online certification

- Experience with cash-basis accounting



Basic Tools



You should be comfortable with:

- Gmail and
 communication

- Google Docs and Google Sheets

- Communication tools (Slack, WhatsApp, or similar)

- Screen recording or video tools (Loom or similar) for async updates



Training Provided

You don't need to know the short-term rental industry to apply. We'll walk you through our client workflows, STR-specific categorization rules, and the SOPs you'll follow. If you know QBO and can follow a checklist, we'll teach you the rest.



Perks

- Flexible schedule

- Stable, long-term engagement with a growing firm

- Opportunity to take on more responsibility as STRbkeep scales



IMPORTANT — To Apply

Submit an application with your resume and a short video (2–3 minutes). Everything below must be spoken in your video — written responses will not substitute.



In your video, please:



1. Address Cheyenne by name.

2. Tell me briefly about your experience — it doesn't have to be bookkeeping. I want to hear what you've done and how you talk about your work.

3. Answer this question on camera: "Describe a time you were working through a task and realized the instructions you had were not enough to move forward correctly. What did you do?"



Applications without a video, or with a pre-recorded generic video, will not be considered.
